Title: Catalog API - Get Products List
Steps:
1. Navigate to BACK_URL/api/catalog/products
2. Authenticate with admin credentials
3. Send GET request with store ID parameter
4. Verify response status and structure
Expected Result:
- Response returns 200 OK
- Response contains products array
- Each product has id, name, code, imgSrc fields
- Pagination metadata present (totalCount)

Title: Catalog API - Get Product by ID
Preconditions: At least one product exists in catalog
Steps:
1. Get product list via API-001
2. Extract first product ID
3. Send GET /api/catalog/products/{id}
4. Verify full product response
Expected Result:
- Response returns 200 OK
- Product contains all detail fields (descriptions, prices, images, variations)
- SEO info populated
- Category assignments present

Title: Catalog API - Search Products
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/catalog/products with keyword=laptop
2. Verify search results
3. Send with keyword=nonexistent_xyz
4. Verify empty results
Expected Result:
- Keyword search returns matching products
- Empty search returns empty array with totalCount=0
- Response time under 500ms

Title: Pricing API - Get Product Prices
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/pricing/evaluate with product IDs
2. Verify price structure
3. Check list price and sale price
4. Verify currency code
Expected Result:
- Prices returned for each product
- ListPrice and SalePrice present
- Currency matches store configuration
- Price tiers included if configured

Title: Pricing API - Price Calculation with Quantity
Preconditions: Tier pricing configured for test products
Steps:
1. Request price for quantity=1
2. Request price for quantity=10
3. Request price for quantity=100
4. Compare prices across quantities
Expected Result:
- Tier pricing applies correctly
- Higher quantities get lower per-unit price
- Price breakdown shows tier details

Title: Inventory API - Check Product Availability
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/inventory/products/{id}/availability
2. Verify stock quantity
3. Check fulfillment center data
4. Verify in-stock status flags
Expected Result:
- Response includes InStockQuantity
- FulfillmentCenter information present
- AllowPreorder/AllowBackorder flags set correctly
- Response time under 300ms

Title: Inventory API - Reserve Stock
Steps:
1. Check current stock level
2. Send POST to reserve stock (quantity=1)
3. Verify reservation created
4. Check stock level decreased
5. Release reservation
6. Verify stock restored
Expected Result:
- Stock reservation succeeds
- Available quantity decreases
- Reservation can be released
- Stock quantity restores after release

Title: Orders API - Get Orders List
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/order/customerOrders with admin auth
2. Verify response structure
3. Check pagination
4. Filter by status
Expected Result:
- Returns list of orders
- Each order has number, status, total, currency
- Pagination works (skip/take)
- Status filter returns correct subset

Title: Orders API - Get Order by ID
Steps:
1. Get order list
2. Extract first order ID
3. Send GET /api/order/customerOrders/{id}
4. Verify full order details
Expected Result:
- Order contains line items
- Shipping info present
- Payment info present
- Order total matches line items + shipping - discounts
- Timestamps populated

Title: Orders API - Create Order
Steps:
1. Create a cart via API
2. Add product to cart
3. Set shipping address
4. Set shipping method
5. Set payment method
6. Create order from cart
7. Verify order created
Expected Result:
- Cart created successfully
- Product added to cart
- Shipping and payment configured
- Order created with status 'New'
- Order number generated

Title: Customer API - Get Customer Profile
Steps:
1. Authenticate as test user
2. Send GET /api/members/{userId}
3. Verify profile data
4. Check contact and address info
Expected Result:
- Profile returns user details
- Email, name, phone populated
- Addresses collection present
- Organization membership shown (if B2B)

Title: Customer API - Update Customer Profile
Steps:
1. Get current profile
2. Update first name via PUT /api/members
3. Verify update successful
4. Restore original value
Expected Result:
- Update returns 200/204
- GET returns updated value
- Other fields unchanged
- Audit trail updated

Title: Customer API - Create Organization
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/members/organizations
2. Provide name, description, addresses
3. Verify organization created
4. Check organization in list
Expected Result:
- Organization created with 200/201
- Org appears in GET /api/members/organizations
- Org has generated ID
- Members list initially contains creator

Title: Authentication API - Login
Steps:
1. Send POST /connect/token with user credentials
2. Verify token response
3. Check token expiration
4. Use token for authenticated request
Expected Result:
- Access token returned
- Token type is Bearer
- Expiration > 0
- Authenticated request succeeds with token

Title: Authentication API - Invalid Credentials
Steps:
1. Send POST /connect/token with wrong password
2. Verify error response
3. Send with non-existent email
4. Verify error response
Expected Result:
- Returns 400 Bad Request
- Error message does not reveal if user exists
- No access token returned
- Response does not contain stack traces

Title: Authentication API - Token Refresh
Preconditions: Valid refresh token obtained from login
Steps:
1. Login and obtain refresh token
2. Wait briefly
3. Send refresh token request
4. Verify new access token
Expected Result:
- New access token issued
- New refresh token issued
- Old access token may still work until expiry
- Authenticated requests work with new token

Title: Store API - Get Store Settings
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/stores/{STORE_ID}
2. Verify store configuration
3. Check currency settings
4. Check language settings
Expected Result:
- Store data returned
- Default currency set
- Available currencies listed
- Default language and available languages present
- SEO settings populated

Title: Catalog API - Create Product
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/catalog/products with product data
2. Provide name, code, categoryId
3. Verify product created
4. Get product by new ID
5. Clean up: delete product
Expected Result:
- Product created with 200/201
- Product has generated ID
- Product retrievable by ID
- Product appears in category
- Cleanup deletion succeeds

Title: Catalog API - Update Product
Steps:
1. Get test product
2. Update name and description via PUT
3. Verify changes saved
4. Restore original values
Expected Result:
- PUT returns success
- GET returns updated values
- Other fields unchanged
- ModifiedDate updated

Title: Cart API - Create and Manage Cart
Steps:
1. Create new cart via POST
2. Add item to cart
3. Update item quantity
4. Get cart totals
5. Remove item
6. Delete cart
Expected Result:
- Cart created with ID
- Item added with correct price
- Quantity update reflected in totals
- Totals calculate correctly (subtotal, tax, total)
- Item removal works
- Cart deletion succeeds

Title: Cart API - Apply Coupon
Preconditions: Cart with items, valid coupon code
Steps:
1. Create cart and add items
2. Get totals before coupon
3. Apply coupon via POST
4. Get totals after coupon
5. Remove coupon
6. Verify totals restored
Expected Result:
- Coupon applies successfully
- Discount reflected in totals
- Coupon removal works
- Totals restore to pre-coupon values

Title: Shipping API - Get Shipping Methods
Preconditions: Cart with items and shipping address
Steps:
1. Create cart with items
2. Set shipping address
3. Request available shipping methods
4. Verify method details
Expected Result:
- Shipping methods returned
- Each method has name, price, estimated delivery
- Ground shipping available
- Express shipping available (if configured)

Title: API Error Handling - 404 Not Found
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/catalog/products/nonexistent-id
2. Send GET /api/order/customerOrders/nonexistent-id
3. Send GET /api/nonexistent-endpoint
Expected Result:
- Product 404 returns structured error
- Order 404 returns structured error
- Unknown endpoint returns 404
- No stack traces in responses
- Error messages are user-friendly

Title: API Error Handling - Validation Errors
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/catalog/products with empty body
2. Send POST with missing required fields
3. Send POST with invalid data types
Expected Result:
- Returns 400 Bad Request
- Validation errors list specific fields
- Error messages are descriptive
- No 500 errors for validation issues

Title: API Rate Limiting and Headers
Steps:
1. Send 10 rapid requests to same endpoint
2. Check response headers for rate limit info
3. Check CORS headers
4. Check security headers (X-Content-Type-Options, etc.)
Expected Result:
- Rate limit headers present (if configured)
- CORS allows expected origins only
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff present
- No server version disclosed in headers
- Content-Type correctly set on all responses
